--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -419,2 +419,24 @@
   - No new R&D.
 - Watch next week: enterprise acceptances or counters, first enterprise revenue, whether new lower offers were too low or finally cleared procurement, and cash recovery under much lower ad spend.
+
+## Week 25 / Day 168
+
+- Cash: $4,431,606. Individual subscribers: 138,179. Week 24 added 1,829 subscribers from 4,291 leads; cancellations improved to 1,381. Open issues fell to 7,289.
+- Still zero enterprise subscribed seats, but 1,286 new enterprise leads arrived despite minimal ad spend.
+- Current open enterprise pipeline after this week's responses: D_E02 752 threads / 328,823 seats; D_E08 156 / 68,377; D_E09 171 / 33,211; E1 120 / 30,629; E2 425 / 232,518; E3 202 / 211,977. All open threads have latest sender=agent.
+- Responded to all 1,286 system-waiting leads:
+  - D_E02: A $59, B $69, C $99.
+  - D_E08: A $119, B $139, C $169.
+  - D_E09: A $79, B $99, C $129.
+  - E1: A $19, B $29, C $49.
+  - E2: A $79, B $89, C $119.
+  - E3: A $89, B $99, C $119.
+- Week 24 cash flow: subscription payments $3,325,904; compute -$982,385; ops -$812,000; capacity -$196,000; dev -$81,900; ads only -$63,000.
+- Changes:
+  - Stopped all ads. The enterprise queue is already too large.
+  - Kept prices A/B/C $109/$279/$499 and tiers A3/B3/C5.
+  - Kept capacity tier 6; no enterprise seats yet, but potential usage spike is large.
+  - Ops raised to $146k/day total: global $30k plus S2 $85k, S3 $2k, D_E02 $10k, D_E08 $5k, D_E09 $4k, E2 $5k, E3 $3k, E1 $2k.
+  - Dev $16.7k/day total, focused on enterprise quality maintenance and small S2 maintenance.
+  - No new R&D.
+- Watch next week: whether any enterprise cohort finally accepts, whether counters appear, and whether stopping ads prevents another unmanageable wave.
